name: Bhai Harjit & Gurdeep Singh (Delhi)
body: Bhai Harjit Singh, Bhai Gurdeep Singh and Bhai Gurmeet Singh are a ‘jatha’ made up of three brothers who adhere to the teachings of their elders, which is a rarity in these times. They are the nephews of the renowned Bhai Santa Singh Ji, who was one of the leading figures in the world of Gurmat Sangeet during his time. Bhai Santa Singh Ji was one of Bhai Sain Ditta Ji’s best students and it is said that when Bhai Santa Singh Ji completed his training, Bhai Sain Ditta Ji told him “Santa Singh, I recall all the times that I have been very harsh with you. At times I may have even behaved like a butcher, but I am very happy that after my death, you will be preserving my teachings.” Subsequently, when Bhai Santa Singh Ji was tested during an interview to serve as Hazoori Raagi in Sri Harmandir Sahib, Amritsar, he came up on top of 60 other applicants. He was only 14 years old at that time. The above information was translated from the book ‘Punjab De Parsidh Raagi Rababi’ by Sardar Balbir Singh Kanwal.

Bhai Santa Singh Ji had three other brothers whose names were Bhai Shamsher Singh, Bhai Swaran Singh and Bhai Jabarjang Singh. The brothers used to perform together and after the passing of Bhai Santa Singh Ji, the jatha was fronted by Bhai Shamsher Singh Ji. Bhai Harjit Singh, Bhai Gurdeep Singh and Bhai Gurmeet Singh are the sons of Bhai Swaran Singh Ji. These three brothers received their training from their father and uncles. To further their knowledge, they also went on to learn from Pandit Bansi Lal, who was ‘Gurubhai’ of Pandit Jasraj Ji. Currently, they are the keepers of the tradition of Gurmat Sangeet handed down to them by their father and uncles. They have been serving the Delhi Sikh Gurdwara Management Committee for the past 30 years. Bhai Gurdeep Singh Ji has his own blog on which he has posted some interesting information and recordings as well. Please visit his page at the following link http://hsgsragi.blogspot.com/
If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Harjit Singh and Gurdeep Singh Ji, please contact us.

The large collection of recordings were originally uploaded on the ‘Panth Rattan’ website but for some reason all recordings have been taken down from it. Anyway, please check out their website for other wonderful content http://panthrattan.com/
